12.reason: The condition incorrectly requires both uppercase and lowercase ranges simultaneously, which is impossible for a single character. It should check for same type (all lowercase, all uppercase, or all digits) and that s[i+1] > s[i-1].

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition incorrectly requires both uppercase and lowercase ranges simultaneously, which is impossible for a single character. It should check for same type (all lowercase, all uppercase, or all digits) and that s[i+1] > s[i-1].
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The condition is logically flawed: it requires both uppercase and lowercase range checks simultaneously, which is impossible for a single character. reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Condition incorrectly requires both uppercase and lowercase ranges simultaneously, which is impossible; also missing digit range and index bounds checks (i>0 and i<len-1).

15.reason: The loop should include j <= k to include the last character (e.g., a-d should output b, c, d; currently j<k outputs only b, c, missing d). Also, the expansion should output the starting character before the loop.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop should include j <= k to include the last character (e.g., a-d should output b, c, d; currently j<k outputs only b, c, missing d). Also, the expansion should output the starting character before the loop.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: loop condition j<k excludes the final character of the expansion range; it should be j<=k to correctly include the end character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: The loop bound j<k excludes the last character of the expansion range. It should be j<=k to correctly include s[i+1].

8.reason: [gets is unsafe and deprecated; can cause buffer overflow. Should use fgets instead.]

20.reason: Logical error: After printing the expanded characters, the code fails to skip s[i+1] in the next iteration, causing duplicate output. It should increment i and use continue.
